transcript.text: I'm an automated medical assistant. If this is an emergency, hang up and call 911. Do you consent to continue? (yes/no)
Yes.
What is your main medical concern today?
I have a sore throat.
When did your sore throat start?
2 days ago.
Any fever, chills, or night sweats?
No. I well, yes. I have fever and chills, but no night sweats.
Any difficulty swallowing or breathing?
No.
Any swollen lymph nodes or rash?
Swollen lymph nodes, yes, but no rash.
Have you noticed any cough, nasal congestion, or runny nose along with your sore throat?
No.
Have you tried any treatments or medications for your sore throat so far?
I tried some tea with honey and NyQuil, but nothing really helps.
Have you had any recent exposure to anyone with strep throat or COVID-19?
Yes. My son had a stroke for it last week.
Have you noticed any white patches or pus on your tonsils or throat?
Yes.
Any history of rheumatic fever, heart valve disease, or kidney problems?
No.
How old are you? Please say your age in years.
Do you have any medication allergies? If yes, list them. If no, say no.
Penicillin.
What sex were you assigned at birth? Please say male or female.
Female
Is pregnancy possible? (yes/no/unknown)
No.
Have you ever been told you have kidney disease or significantly reduced kidney function? (yes/no/not sure)
No.
Have you ever been told you have liver disease (such as hepatitis, cirrhosis, fatty liver, or abnormal liver tests)? (yes/no/not sure)
No.

It sounds like you have symptoms consistent with strep throat, which is a bacterial infection that often causes fever, sore throat, and swollen lymph nodes. Because you have a penicillin allergy, medication like azithromycin may be appropriate, but a licensed physician will review your information and decide if prescribing this is right for you. For now, you can use acetaminophen or ibuprofen to help with pain and fever if you can take them safely. If you develop trouble breathing or swallowing, or if your symptoms get worse or don’t improve in the next couple of days, please seek in-person care at an urgent care or emergency room. Since this is a phone visit, no tests were done today, but testing for strep throat may be recommended to confirm the diagnosis. A licensed physician will review your case and follow up as needed.
